mentions.note: Caller (Spencer) asks if TSMC will skyrocket like Micron/NVDA. Cramer: might not skyrocket but will go higher; everyone (Tan, Jensen, others) praises it; no parabolic move yet but potential.
Chip manufacturer essential to AI infrastructure layer. Global capacity constraints driving adoption of optical interconnects.
More business than it can handle. Supply constraint driven by demand from Arm Holdings and others needing chips.
Like it very much; terrific. Q1 report initially down but fundamentals solid — recovered within 48 minutes. Very good situation for AI demand.
Nvidia manufacturing partner; monster quarter validates agentic AI shift from generative; CEO Wei confirms market in command-mode phase favoring Nvidia.
Cramer likes it very much but redirects caller to Nvidia as superior opportunity; TSMC solid but Nvidia down and disliked (contrarian signal).
Reported blowout quarter; guiding $52-56B capex (up 27-37%), higher than Street expected; no supply-demand balance until 2028-2029; demand 'on fire'.
Semiconductor manufacturer; key partner to Nvidia; critical to high-end chip production.
Reported strongest quarter ever with insatiable AI chip demand; spending $50B to keep up with orders; validates hyperscaler ROI and AI durability thesis.
Manufacturing partner of AMD and Nvidia; should be monster quarter but everyone knows it; could help shake off Nvidia sellers if strong results.

Caller Craig calls it amazing tech/manufacturing company addressing energy via 2nm+ chips; Cramer gets up 2am for earnings call, acknowledges magnificent company; owns Nvidia instead (largest TSMC client) for Chapel Trust—no need for redundant Taiwan exposure.
